rationale.R1.1: The critique identifies a key conceptual issue (overgeneralization of results) and proposes a tighter framing aligned with the empirical evidence.
rationale.RX.1: The critique flags uncertainty regarding the generalizability of the findings to other PDEs and types of ensembling.
rationale.RX.3: The critique accurately assesses the scope of the work, focusing on a single PDE and ensemble size.
rationale.RX.4: The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions.
rationale.RX.5: The critique suggests potential improvements to provenance and traceability by providing more details about the baseline methods and ablations.
